A-party website isn't unusual.

There's been kind of reports recently that a lot of LLMs seem to favor citing like G2 or Reddit or just like third-party websites that aren't the brand's website when it comes to talking about a specific brand.

This doesn't mean that like publishing content and like updating your website is like doesn't matter, but because it's still like is part of all the information that the LLM has about your brand, but it does mean that like it's important to make sure that like your G2, for example, or your TrustRadius profile and all of like different review sites that you're using, that all of those are up to date.
For example, if you have any features that you really want customers to know about when they're researching different platforms in an LLM, make sure that you also highlight them.
